commit.message:
Fix weather damage not showing on page refresh after god/tick

Root causes:
1. GET /state lacked Cache-Control: no-store — browsers could cache the
   pre-weather state and serve it on the next page refresh instead of
   fetching the freshly-saved post-weather state from the server.

2. The UI's poll() fetch used the default cache mode, meaning the browser's
   HTTP cache (including any stale ETag-matched response) could be used
   instead of always hitting the server.

3. The scheduler's lastTickAt was only updated inside runTick(), not by
   external HTTP tick handlers. If TICK_CRON was set to a short interval,
   the scheduler could fire very soon after a god/tick and — in a Cosmos DB
   environment where async reads can interleave — read the pre-god/tick state
   and overwrite the freshly saved weather damage.

Fixes:
- state.js: add res.set('Cache-Control', 'no-store') to both GET /state
  routes so browsers always fetch a fresh copy on page reload.
- index.html: add cache: 'no-store' to the fetch('/state') call in poll()
  so the browser never uses a cached response.
- scheduler.js: export recordExternalTick() which sets lastTickAt to now,
  giving the scheduler's double-fire guard visibility into manual ticks.
- god.js, tick.js: call recordExternalTick() after each successful save so
  the scheduler's 50-minute guard blocks an immediate follow-up tick that
  could race-overwrite the saved state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix timeline hover reset: preserve snapshot on mouseleave/mousemove

When the timeline scrubber is positioned at a past tick, mousing off the
canvas was unconditionally redrawing the live state (lastCells/lastFlags).

- Add getDisplayCellsAndFlags() helper that returns snapshot data when
  scrubbingIndex >= 0, or live data when in live mode (scrubbingIndex == -1)
- Use it in the mouseleave handler so leaving the canvas restores the
  correct snapshot view instead of jumping to live state
- Use it in the god-mode mousemove RAF callback so hover highlighting is
  also based on the currently displayed snapshot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove AZURE_RECORD_MODE override from Set-CliBuildVariables.ps1 (#7103)

The script unconditionally set AZURE_RECORD_MODE=playback as a pipeline
variable before the test task runs, overriding the AzureRecordMode pipeline
parameter introduced in PR #6893. This caused manually triggered LIVE test
runs to still execute in playback mode.

The record mode is now fully controlled by the AzureRecordMode parameter
chain (release-cli.yml → build-and-test.yml → build-cli.yml).

Fixes #7102

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: correct COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN requirements

Must be a Fine-Grained PAT (not Classic), scoped to 'Public repositories'
with Account permissions → Copilot Requests: Read-only. Classic PATs are
explicitly rejected by the gh-aw runner.

fix(readme): fix pre-commit hooks and regenerate README/TILES from actual skills (#8)

* fix(readme): fix lefthook sync hooks and regenerate README/TILES from actual skills

- Fix lefthook.yml: move readme-sync, yamllint, shellcheck, tessl-lint
  into the commands block (were incorrectly nested at pre-commit level
  and never ran)
- Widen readme-sync trigger glob from .context/audits/*.md to
  {skills/**,cli/**,.context/audits/**} so it fires on any skill change
- Add TILES.md to the files regenerated and staged by readme-sync
- Fix tile-parser to skip private child tiles when parent dir has a
  tile.json (prevents opencode sub-skills appearing as 9 separate tiles)
- Fix trailing double newline in generated README.md causing MD012 errors
- Add languages domain to domain-config.ts for future skill discoverability
- Regenerate README.md and TILES.md: now accurately reflects 48 tiles

commit.message:
Preserve Dapr configuration on container app deployment (#7062)

* Initial plan

* Fix: preserve Dapr configuration during container app deployment

Co-authored-by: spboyer <7681382+spboyer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve Dapr preservation error handling and add 404 test

- Handle 404 (first deploy) explicitly in persistSettings instead of
  swallowing all errors — proceed without persisting when app does not
  exist yet
- Fail on non-404 errors when Dapr preservation is needed to prevent
  silent config wipe (correctness-critical path)
- Add test for first-deploy scenario (GET 404) verifying no Dapr
  config is injected
- Fix pre-existing cspell issues (projectpkg, agentserver)
- Apply go fix modernizations (to.Ptr -> new)

commit.message:
ci: fix release workflow — handle no existing tags

* feat(db): add Prisma schema with User, Profile, Document, NDA models

- User model with role (CREATIVE/COMPANY) and plan enum
- ProfileCreative: fullName, birthDate, birthPlace, codiceFiscale, partitaIva
- ProfileCompany: ragioneSociale, formaGiuridica, sede, SDI, referente
- Document: file metadata + SHA-256 hash for IP timestamping
- NdaAgreement + NdaClause + NdaAgreementClause junction table
- prisma.config.ts for datasource configuration

* chore: update dependencies and config for MVP stack

- Add tRPC, @tanstack/react-query, NextAuth, Prisma, bcryptjs, zod
- Add Shadcn/ui, lucide-react, class-variance-authority
- Update .gitignore for generated prisma client
- Add Shadcn components.json config

* feat(api): add tRPC routers, NextAuth config, and Prisma client

- tRPC context with session + prisma, public + protected procedures
- Auth router: 7 multi-step signup procedures + me query
- Document router: create, list, getById with SHA-256 hash
- NDA router: create, list, getById, accept (clickwrap with IP logging)
- NextAuth credentials provider with JWT strategy
- Prisma client singleton with pg adapter
- TypeScript augmentations for next-auth session types

* feat(api): add NextAuth and tRPC API route handlers

- /api/auth/[...nextauth] — NextAuth handler
- /api/trpc/[trpc] — tRPC fetch adapter handler

* feat(ui): add Shadcn/ui components and SessionProvider

- Accordion, Badge, Button, Card, Checkbox, Input, Label, Separator, Tabs
- NextAuth SessionProvider wrapper for client components

* feat(auth): implement multi-step signup and login flow

- Splash page with rainbow gradient logo + auto-redirect
- Login page: email/password + SPID/CIE layout buttons (disabled)
- Profile selection: Creativo (yellow) / Azienda (blue) cards
- Multi-step signup wizard with progress bar (6 steps creative, 7 company)
- StepSignup, StepPersonalInfo, StepCompanyInfo, StepCompanyReferent
- StepPhone, StepOTP (mock: accepts 123456), StepPlan

* feat(dashboard): add home feed, NDA creation, and document upload

- Home feed with FeedCard component (preview, title, type badge, SHA hash)
- FilterMenu with type filter (Timestamp/NDA/Contract) and sort
- Tabs (Tutti/Inviati/Non inviati) and FAB button
- NDA creation form: title, description, duration selector, clause checklist
- Document upload: drag & drop, client-side SHA-256 hash, title input

* feat(layout): dark mode root layout with TRPCProvider

- Dark mode HTML class + Inter/Geist fonts
- TRPCProvider + SessionProvider wrappers
- Shadcn/ui CSS design tokens (dark theme)
- Splash page with rainbow gradient logo

* chore(docker): add multi-stage Dockerfile and compose

commit.message:
Resolve merge conflict - use credential injection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

🔧 Fix OCR, AI marking, and markscheme detection issues

## Problems Fixed

1. **Environment variables not loading in dev**
   - Added dotenv to automatically load .env file
   - API keys (MISTRAL_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY) now properly loaded

2. **Automatic markscheme detection disabled**
   - Re-enabled detectAndFetchMarkscheme() function
   - Implemented full detection flow with Sonar + Exa fallback
   - Auto-saves detected markscheme to submission

3. **Poor error handling and logging**
   - Enhanced OCR client with detailed logging (character counts, caching info)
   - Enhanced AI grading with step-by-step logging
   - Added comprehensive detection logging with emoji indicators
   - Better error messages for debugging

## Changes

- dev.ts: Added dotenv config() call
- src/lib/ocr/client.ts: Enhanced error handling + logging
- src/lib/ai/client.ts: Enhanced error handling + logging
- src/lib/ai/detection.ts: Re-enabled with comprehensive logging
- src/server/api/submissions.ts: Enabled /detect-markscheme endpoint
- package.json: Added dotenv dependency

## Testing

✅ All systems verified locally:
- Health check returns all services as true
- API keys properly loaded in development

feat: Issue CRUD UI components (#28)

Implements complete UI for Issue CRUD operations:

Pages:
- Issues/Index.razor: Paginated list with status/category filters
- Issues/Create.razor: Form to create new issues with validation
- Issues/Details.razor: View issue details, change status, delete
- Issues/Edit.razor: Update existing issue

Shared Components:
- StatusBadge.razor: Colored badge for issue status
- CategoryBadge.razor: Colored badge for issue category
- DeleteConfirmationModal.razor: Reusable confirmation dialog
- Pagination.razor: Reusable pagination component

Services:
- LookupService.cs: Service for fetching categories/statuses

Features:
- Full CRUD operations via IssueService
- Tailwind CSS styling with dark mode support
- Loading and error states
- Form validation with DataAnnotations
- Status change directly from details page
- Soft delete with confirmation modal

Closes #22

commit.message:
feat(web): Dark mode and color theme support - Phase 2 (#18)

Implement theming system with dark mode toggle and color scheme selection:

- Add ThemeProvider.razor component with JS interop for state management
- Add ThemeToggle.razor component with sun/moon icons and dropdown
- Add ColorSchemeSelector.razor component with blue/red/green/yellow schemes
- Create wwwroot/js/theme.js for localStorage persistence
- Define CSS custom properties for color scheme variables (oklch color space)
- Update MainLayout.razor with theme components and dark mode classes
- Update App.razor with theme.js script (loads before render to prevent flash)
- Use Tailwind's dark: variant for responsive dark mode styling
- Store preferences in localStorage (theme-mode, theme-color)
- Respect prefers-color-scheme when set to 'system' mode

Closes #16

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
